SEARCH
Package edu.yu.cs.com3810.models

Class AutomationRule

java.lang.Object
edu.yu.cs.com3810.models.AutomationRule
public class AutomationRule extends Object
Represents a user-defined automation rule.

An automation rule consists of a trigger and an action:

  • Trigger: a specific event type from a specific input device type (e.g., DOORBELL_RING from a DOORBELL).
  • Action: a DeviceAction to issue to a specific target device when the trigger fires.

Rules are stored by the Automation Hub and evaluated each time an event is received on the TopicType.EVENT topic.

Example: "When my front doorbell rings, turn on the living room lights."

  • Constructor Details

    • AutomationRule

      public AutomationRule(String ruleName, DeviceType triggerDeviceType, String triggerEventType, String triggerDeviceId, String targetDeviceId, DeviceAction targetAction)
      Constructs a new AutomationRule.
      Parameters:
      ruleName - a user-supplied name for this rule
      triggerDeviceType - the type of device that produces the trigger event
      triggerEventType - the event type string that activates this rule
      triggerDeviceId - the specific device ID required to trigger this rule, or null to match any device of the given type
      targetDeviceId - the ID of the device to send the action command to
      targetAction - the action to issue to the target device

  • Method Details

    • getRuleId

      public String getRuleId()
      Returns:
      the unique ID of this rule

    • getRuleName

      public String getRuleName()
      Returns:
      the human-readable name of this rule

    • getTriggerDeviceType

      public DeviceType getTriggerDeviceType()
      Returns:
      the device type that triggers this rule

    • getTriggerEventType

      public String getTriggerEventType()
      Returns:
      the event type string that triggers this rule

    • getTriggerDeviceId

      public String getTriggerDeviceId()
      Returns the specific device ID required to trigger this rule.
      Returns:
      a device ID, or null if any device of the matching type qualifies

    • getTargetDeviceId

      public String getTargetDeviceId()
      Returns:
      the ID of the device to command when this rule fires

    • getTargetAction

      public DeviceAction getTargetAction()
      Returns:
      the action to issue to the target device

    • isActive

      public boolean isActive()
      Returns:
      true if this rule is currently active and will be evaluated

    • setActive

      public void setActive(boolean active)
      Enables or disables this automation rule.
      Parameters:
      active - true to activate the rule, false to deactivate it
